props.message:
PLINTH I

"The breath of Lirien moves
through us all —
through the merchant's tally,
through the watchman's pace,
through the long quiet
of those who do not speak
but listen, and listen,
and are made larger by listening."

PLINTH II

"What is given is not lost.
What is hoarded thins
in the hand that holds it."

PLINTH III

"Three roads, one breath.
Three peoples, one ground.
Older than the agreement,
the agreement."

PLINTH IV

"The bell does not ring the hour.
She reminds the hour to ring itself."

FRAGMENT (author lost)

"We were a people of the held breath.
Then we learned to let it out.
The world did not end.
It became a song, briefly,
and then a quieter thing,
and then ours."

FRAGMENT

"Of the three Sigils,
the kindest is Sharing —
though the kindness wears
the shape of work."

POSTED

The bell rings on the hour, and one
additional time when nobody is watching.
This is tradition. Do not investigate.

INSCRIPTION

"The Maker walks the realm in many shapes.
Most we will never recognise. Some
will leave the change of a coin
and be gone before we count it."

ATTRIBUTED TO TELVA

"I have not seen Lirien.
I have stood in places
where she had recently been,
and the stones were warmer."

FROM A LONGER PIECE

"The Sprawl is contested ground.
Contested ground is shared ground.
Sharing is what we do here, even
when we forget we are doing it,
even when we are arguing about it,
even — most of all — then."

props.open_message: Ten pebbles, gathered for the courtyard's children. The Sharers won't mind.
A Piece of Heart. Tucked beside the plinth like a private offering.
A bottled wisp. A small kindness, kept under glass.

root.speaker: Poet Telva
root.text: Walker. The plinths are warm tonight. Would you hear a verse?

verse.speaker: Poet Telva
verse.text:
"The hand that holds nothing
is the hand that is full of all things.
The hand that grasps
is the hand that is empty
of the air it once cupped
without knowing it was holding it."

thanks.speaker: Poet Telva
thanks.text: Lirien's breath. You are kind. The plinth is kinder. Walk on, walker.

bye.speaker: Poet Telva
bye.text: Walk well. The bell will ring soon.

root.speaker: Historian Pren
root.text: A walker. Good. I have been talking to the plinths and they are not improving as listeners.

choices.label: Why does any of this matter?

sprawl.speaker: Historian Pren
sprawl.text: Built in the third long-quiet, after the second forgetting, before the third. Three clans came together not because they liked one another but because the wind here was kinder than where they came from.

sharers.speaker: Historian Pren
sharers.text: We are the cultural ward. We tend verse, plinth, bell. The Binds find us insufferable. We find ourselves slightly insufferable. The Locals do not find us at all.

bell.speaker: Historian Pren
bell.text: Older than the courtyard. Possibly older than the Sprawl. Cael speaks of the bell as kin. I have stopped arguing. The bell does not seem to mind either way.

matter.speaker: Historian Pren
matter.text: *(a small, real pause)*  ...That, walker, is the one thing I have never had a satisfying answer to. I keep teaching anyway. Make of it what you will.

root.speaker: Olm
root.text: *(in a Bind's clipped tone)* You're staring. Yes, I'm Bind. No, I'm not selling. I came here to mock the verses.

and.speaker: Olm
and.text: And the first one was awful. *Truly* awful. Then I read another. ...And another. Then I sat down. Then a turn of the moon went by.

still.speaker: Olm
still.text: *(quieter)* My ledger sits on its shelf. My stall is still open. My second-cousin keeps the chips. I keep coming back. I do not know why. ...That is not honest. I know exactly why.

bye.speaker: Olm
bye.text: Walk well. Don't tell the Guild.

root.speaker: Bell-keeper Cael
root.text: Walker. The bell is well today. She has been quiet a long time, and she is enjoying the quiet.

choices.label: How long have you tended her?

bell.speaker: Bell-keeper Cael
bell.text: She is older than her tower, older than the courtyard, older than the agreement that brought the courtyard here. I do not know who first hung her. I do not need to.

long.speaker: Bell-keeper Cael
long.text: Long enough. My grandmother tended her. Her grandmother tended her. The line has been thin once or twice. We always come back to her. She is patient.

bye.speaker: Bell-keeper Cael
bye.text: Walk well. Listen for her on the hour.

root.speaker: Apprentice Nye
root.text: Oh — hello. I'm — I'm practicing. Telva says the plinth lines come easier if you say them aloud. They don't. Yet.

try.speaker: Apprentice Nye
try.text: Um — "The hand that holds — that holds —" ...the hand that holds *something*. I forget the rest. Telva will know. She always knows.

shy.speaker: Apprentice Nye
shy.text: I'm not — I'm just — quiet. Quiet is also Sharer work, Cael says. The bell is quiet most of the time too.

bye.speaker: Apprentice Nye
bye.text: Walk well! ...I'll get the line right next time.

root.speaker: The Fortune Teller
root.text:
*She turns over a smooth stone. Each side carries a word that wasn't there a moment ago.*

The realm has prepared a saying for you. Listen.

f2.speaker: The Fortune Teller
f2.text: "The early bird gets the worm, but the second mouse gets the cheese."

f3.speaker: The Fortune Teller
f3.text: "You will find what you seek, after you stop seeking it."

f4.speaker: The Fortune Teller
f4.text: "All that is gold does not glitter; not all who wander are lost."

f5.speaker: The Fortune Teller
f5.text: "Today is the first day of the rest of your loop."

f6.speaker: The Fortune Teller
f6.text: "Beware the man of one book. Especially if it is this one."

f7.speaker: The Fortune Teller
f7.text: "To err is human; to forgive, divine; to debug, neither."

f8.speaker: The Fortune Teller
f8.text: "A penguin in motion stays in motion. A penguin at rest is also in motion. (The realm spins.)"

f9.speaker: The Fortune Teller
f9.text: "The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is after you finish this errand."

f10.speaker: The Fortune Teller
f10.text: "You will meet a tall stranger. They will be a sign."

f11.speaker: The Fortune Teller
f11.text: "Remember: every door was once a wall someone got tired of."

f12.speaker: The Fortune Teller
f12.text: "Help is on the way. It may be slower than you would prefer."

bye.speaker: The Fortune Teller
bye.text: *She pockets the stone.* Come back when the next one is ready.

root.speaker: The Word-Hoarder
root.text:
I know every word. *he taps a stack of scrolls thicker than his arm*

I never use them all. Most of them are waiting. One of them is probably for you.

which.speaker: The Word-Hoarder
which.text: I cannot tell you. Knowing which word is yours would deplete it. Take your time. You will hear it.
